layers.name: Electron Main Process
layers.description: Window lifecycle, IPC routing, tray, provider snapshots, and overlay coordinator logic.

layers.name: Renderer UI
layers.description: Desktop bar, HUD, settings, popovers, and renderer styles.

layers.name: Foreground Window Detection
layers.description: Platform-specific foreground sampling, shell filtering, and overlay noise detection.

layers.name: Provider And Protocol Layer
layers.description: Usage events, provider health, collectors, SDK, and import surfaces.

layers.name: Adapters And Extensions
layers.description: Browser, VS Code, Hermes, and adapter packaging/integration code.

layers.name: Verification And Release Tooling
layers.description: Regression tests, packaging checks, release evidence, and validation scripts.

layers.name: Configuration
layers.description: Project manifests, workflow files, package metadata, and config assets.

tour.title: Start at the Electron shell
tour.description: Begin with the main process and preload bridge that create windows and expose safe renderer IPC.

tour.title: Follow transparent overlay arbitration
tour.description: Inspect how foreground sampling, owned-window filtering, and hitbox separation prevent focus theft.

tour.title: Read the renderer surfaces
tour.description: Compare desktop bar, HUD display, trust popover, settings page, and shared styles.

tour.title: Trace provider usage data
tour.description: Move from protocol and provider modules into the UI that presents quota/capacity state.

tour.title: Check regression guards
tour.description: Use the focused static test suites to understand which transparent-window and performance regressions are guarded.
